g.text: Streaming Board: 2D chunked sliding window over a scratch file
Auto-promotes when an --initial-board run exceeds --max-board-memory. Keeps RAM bounded by paging
a small chunk of cells in and out of a binary scratch file on disk.
Row payloads (height x row_bytes, fixed stride)
2 bits per cell, 4 cells per byte
for boundary bytes so bits belonging
to cells outside the write range are
Lifecycle: scratch lives in --working-dir (default OS temp), named gol-scratch-<run-id>-<random>.bin so concurrent runs never collide.
Auto-deleted on successful completion. Preserved on any failure (panic / Ctrl-C / SIGKILL / I/O error) for debugging.
Memory cap (--max-board-memory) governs chunk RAM only, using size_of::<CellState>() per cell. Disk packing is irrelevant to RAM accounting.
